calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jun-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Calvin Love, Esq. for Assignee/Judgment Creditor, Credilogical Systems, LLC
Cassius Wilson, Judgment Debtor
Maria A. Torrenao, Court reporter, CSR #8600

Judgment Debtor, Cassius Wilson, contests the Court's tentative ruling. Court notes that the Judgment debtor did not provide proper notice to contest this matter today. Since the Judgment Creditor is present, the matter is heard. Argument is held. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ACA RECEIVABLES CO.,INC.,LLC HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

Based on the evidence submitted by the judgment creditor, the debtor's claim of exemption is denied, and the levying officer is directed to release any earnings held to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. The judgment creditor shows that the debtor was personally served with the summons and complaint on January 31, 2006 by a registered process server (as she has admitted); that a default judgment was entered against her on April 10, 2006 in the amount of $2,076.16; that the debtor had numerous conversations with the creditor during the period 2005-2008, before and after entry of judgment, relating to paying off the debt; and that during 2002, when the debt was incurred (and at other times), she was living in Sacramento County, contradicting her representation to the Court that she never lived in Sacramento. Further, the court's docket reflects that in January 2016 and again in March 2020, the debtor was served with notice of the creditor's application for renewal of the judgment, and that on neither occasion did she move to vacate or modify the renewal.

Orders are signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-17-2021 Continued To Jun-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Calvin Love appearing for Plaintiff;
Carolyn Evans, Defendant/Self-Represented Litigant.

PLAINTIFF ACA RECEIVABLES CO.,INC.,LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Matter called. ARGUMENT/DISCUSSIONS are held.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the matter is continued to June 1,2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302 for a further hearing.

Mr. Love is to provide the Court with a copy of Ms. Evans' letter/declaration that he received by email and any additional documentation on his end that supports the creditor's position. The additional documentation for the Court is to be submitted by May 27, 2021. The Court requested that the documentation include where Carolyn Evans was served, what address the phone bill was sent to, and the communications from Carolyn Evans requesting to set up payments.

The Court states that Ms. Love has stated that she never lived in Sacramento, that it wasn't her address, that she thought these weren't hers, and that if it is a default judgment that it is Mr. Love's client's obligation to get to the bottom of it.

Ms. Evans requests an explanation. The Court states that it is having Mr. Love examine the document that she submitted and look into this further/do an investigation.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-03-2021 Continued To May-17-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. R. Thomas Griffith appearing for Plaintiff's counsel;
Carolyn Evans, Defendant/Self-Represented Litigant.

PLAINTIFF ACA RECEIVABLES CO.,INC.,LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Matter called. ARGUMENT/DISCUSSIONS are held.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the matter is continued to May 17, 2021 at 9;30 a.m. in Dept. 302.

In support of Ms. Evans' argument that she did not reside in Sacramento and that this is not her debt she agrees to submit a declaration signed under penalty of perjury to Mr. Griffiths. The declaration should state under penalty of perjury that she did not reside in Sacramento between November 13, 2002 to 2005. Ms. Evans is to include documentation to support where she lived at that time. The declaration and supporting documentation should be emailed to Mr. Griffiths at: TomGriffith@cox.net. Mr. Griffiths will forward the information to his client.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
